title: almost didnt understand the last 5 episodes
display_title: almost didnt understand the last 5 episodes
wikitext:
[]: almost didnt understand the last 5 episodes             Quote Post by RogueEvangalion » Mon Jan 25, 2010 1:26 pm Is it just me or was the writer on crack when he wrote the last 5 episodes cause I almost didnt get it, the whole life is based on your perception I understood but whats with the blank world and the world when Asuka is Shinji's childhood friend, and the part that srewed me up the most was why did Shinji have that weird little room were he was shut of from the rest of the world and why did he have to take that journey through the recesses of his mind yo figuire out that life is based on your perception, WHY?

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Mon Jan 25, 2010 1:43 pm Something about this thread seems straaaaaangely familiar ! And I'm pretty sure it's just you. I would say that watching the director's cut episodes and End of Evangelion might help you understand a bit more of why the last few episodes unfold the way they do, but considering this thread and your other one I'm...not so sure that would help you.

[]:             Quote Post by RogueEvangalion » Mon Jan 25, 2010 1:52 pm I guess so but it is a bit strange, he could have made it easier to follow

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Jan 25, 2010 1:53 pm The series shits vehicles of relaying information to the audience at this point. I think much of it was due to budget/time cuts and creative differences between the studio and the TV networks carrying the show. Things that were portrayed in a very literal sense started to be covered in a abstract, internal sense. Which, by the way, is much cheaper to produced under such a tight schedule the networks were giving the studio at the time.

[]: Re: almost didnt understand the last 5 episodes             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jan 25, 2010 1:59 pm >Is it just me It's you >or was the writer on crack when he wrote the last 5 episodes He had been going through major depression and was using the series as art therapy. >whats with the blank world Running out of budget, they had to improvise how to tell the ending >and the world when Asuka is Shinji's childhood friend Shinji seeing for himself that he can be happy without all the giant robot stuff >why did Shinji have that weird little room were he was shut of from the rest of the world Because he is a metaphor for the shut-in otaku of Japan >and why did he have to take that journey through the recesses of his mind yo figuire out that life is based on your perception It's a way of making a talking cure somewhat worth watching. >WHY? Because Now go watch End of Evangelion .

[]: Re: almost didnt understand the last 5 episodes             Quote Post by Merridian » Mon Jan 25, 2010 1:59 pm Just rewatch the series, man. The things you've mentioned are pretty self-explanatory if you just pay attention. They flat-out STATE verbatim what's going on during those scenes, so I don't see what the problem is. Also, nonlinearity and incoherency =/= "on crack"

[]: Re: almost didnt understand the last 5 episodes             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Jan 25, 2010 2:13 pm RogueEvangalion wrote: Is it just me or was the writer on crack when he wrote the last 5 episodes cause I almost didnt get it

[]: Re: almost didnt understand the last 5 episodes             Quote Post by VoidEater » Tue Jan 26, 2010 4:50 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Shinji seeing for himself that he can be happy without all the giant robot stuff

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Wed Jan 27, 2010 12:12 am or was the writer on crack when he wrote the last 5 episodes

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Wed Jan 27, 2010 5:28 pm It's just you. And when people say "it's like they wrote this on drugs", do they actually know what writing on drugs looks like? I don't actually think crack would produce such an effect. It's actually really easy to write this way without any chemical influences at all.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Thu Feb 04, 2010 6:46 pm Those episodes took me a few times to understand them. Of course, The End of Evangelion was better explained than the final 2 TV episodes (Even after many months, I still don't understand them).

[]:             Quote Post by Halicat » Mon Feb 08, 2010 9:07 am Concerning the drug bit, it's just you. What I find with Eva is that all the answers you want to find are right there in front of you, it just doesn't tell you what they are. So watching it several times over and reading up on what other people think should explain it better and then you'll find it makes perfect sense, even if you haven't cracked every element to it [Which, I believe, would be impossible to do.]
